item.title: Topology
item.description: Definition A topology, on a set S, \mathcal{T} is a collection defined as follows: Must contain S and \emptyset Must be closed under finite intersections Must be closed under unions A set which is a member of the topology is called an open set Topological Space A topological space is a set and Topol...

item.title: Classification of Boolean Algebras
item.description: Boolean Algebras are the same as a certain class of topological space, Stone Spaces.

item.title: Operation
item.description: Operation An operation is a function which takes a predefined finite number of inputs and whos domain is a power of its codomain.

item.title: Sets
item.description: A set is a collection of objects.

item.title: Xor
item.description: Xor is a logical binary operation which takes two values of the set of \mathbb{Z} / 2 and applies the “exclusive or” function which only returns 1 if both operands are different and will otherwise return 0.

item.title: And
item.description: And is a logical binary operation which takes two values of the set of \mathbb{Z} / 2 and applies the “conjunction” function which only returns 1 only if both operands are 1 and otherwise returns 0.

item.title: Biconditional
item.description: Bicondition, or bicon, or if and only if, or iff is a logical binary operation which takes two values of the set of \mathbb{Z} / 2 and applies the biconditional function which only returns 1 if both operands are the same and will otherwise return 0.

item.title: Implies
item.description: Implies is a logical binary operation which takes two values of the set of \mathbb{Z} / 2 and applies the “implies” function which only returns 0 if True implies False.
